content.claim: Finland's formally untracked comprehensive system has accommodated informal selection through 'emphasized classes' (since 1990s) using testing and prior achievement, creating within-school segregation by performance level, with class-level performance differentials visible in data.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Mechanism documented as practice but causal effect on equity outcomes separate from system-wide trends not isolated
Scale of emphasized-class enrollment relative to total student population not quantified in notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
